# `liber:input@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:input` package (`input.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem input package - pointer and input event services.

## Types

### record `pointer-event`

One pointer event mapped to the text-cell grid: the cursor's column and row, and the button bitmask (bit 0 left, bit 1 right, bit 2 middle). The canonical typed form of a mouse position - the framebuffer console grid coordinates, never raw device units (a pixel form would only ever be a rendering of these cells).

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `col` | `u16` |  |
| `row` | `u16` |  |
| `buttons` | `u8` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `input`

InputService: typed pointer/button events from the virtio-input pointer device, mapped to the text-cell grid. `subscribe` hands back a wait-drained event stream of the recent pointer events (the bounded-snapshot form; a continuously live stream is a later refinement). The device is reached as a capability, never as ambient input access - the plumbing a future TUI consumes.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `subscribe` | `subscribe() -> stream<pointer-event>` |  |
